answer_key.9: begin (happening X begins at time T)

instructions: BLIND style spot-check (fork F-KN-A, design doc section 2.3): read the 10 definitions below WITHOUT looking at answer_key. For each, judge: (a) does it read as ordinary dictionary-register English (not NSM-style controlled language)? (b) is it a fair definition a general dictionary could print? Record verdicts, then unblind.

items.definition: an embrace in which one person wraps both arms around another and draws them together briefly; given to express warm affection toward the recipient; the gesture comforts and pleases the person receiving it, strengthening the bond between the two
no longer living; having permanently ceased all vital functions; incapable of any further action or awareness
able to be seen; open to view, so that anyone who looks in the right direction perceives it
an adult female human being; a grown person, no longer a girl, who has reached maturity; individuals of this sex can bear children from their own bodies

a domesticated canine widely kept by households; it barks loudly when anything stirs nearby, alerting owners at a distance; renowned for loyalty, it forms warm bonds with its keepers, and most owners return the affection; breeds vary greatly in size

a saved pointer that records the location of a document or page; created by a user so that the material can be reopened quickly; it carries a short descriptive title and spares the reader from searching again

a male parent; the man whose union with the mother led to a child's conception and arrival; such a parent commonly shares the home with his offspring over many years, providing care, support, and guidance while the youngsters grow
to create or produce an object; through deliberate work something is brought into existence that was absent before
morally improper to perform; an action of this sort causes harm or offense, and people condemn those who choose to do it
to start occurring; the point at which a process or activity commences; prior to that point the activity was absent
